summary:
For an irreparable rotator cuff tear, total shoulder replacement is the appropriate treatment since native tissue cannot be reattached and symptoms persist without restoring joint function. This approach aligns with standard orthopedic care, which prioritizes functional outcomes over attempts at tendon repair in such severe cases. 

The summary above contains redundant content; please revise accordingly while strictly adhering to two-sentence requirement.

For an irreparable rotator cuff tear, total shoulder replacement is the optimal treatment to restore function when native tissue cannot be repaired. It replaces failed joint structures and alleviates pain by addressing underlying pathophysiology beyond what can be achieved with tendon-focused therapies.  

Please ensure your final output meets both requirements—strictly two sentences—and avoids redundancy.

For an irreparable rotator cuff tear, total shoulder replacement is the standard treatment to provide functional recovery when tendon repair is impossible. This procedure effectively manages persistent pain and loss of mobility caused by advanced joint deterioration incompatible with reconstructive efforts.
